themes.topic: Innolight reports increased 800G demand and broad supply chain shortages
themes.summary: Innolight's investor relations takeaways indicate significantly increased 800G demand and broad supply chain shortages across optical/electrical chips, PCBs, and other module materials, with silicon photonics penetration continuing to trend upward.

mentions.text:
TLDR of Innolight investor relations takeaways:

1. "Overall, 1.6T market demand has not contracted; instead, 800G demand has increased significantly compared with previous expectations"

Prob most important takeaway as a whole was 800G demand revision (also longer tail demand).

themes.topic: AAOI Texas manufacturing expansion for optical transceivers
themes.summary: AAOI announced the commencement of its Texas manufacturing expansion, adding nearly 400,000 sq ft for 800G/1.6T optical transceiver production, targeting significant capacity increases by mid-2027.

mentions.text:
Really positive to see $AAOI announcing their Texas capacity expansion earlier today.

We've known about their expansion plans for a while now.

But it overall supports their proposed 800G/1.6T expansion timelines:
- Q1 2026: 100k monthly capacity 
- End 2026: 650k+ 
- End 2027:

Two different capacity expansion announcements today across $TSEM and $AAOI.

Tower Semi, supported by the Japanese government, announced expansion of its 300mm Silicon Photonics (SiPho), Silicon Germanium (SiGe), and advanced packaging capabilities. 

Targeting $1.2B net profit,

$AAOI just broke ground on nearly 400,000 sq ft of new manufacturing in Pearland, Texas.

All of it dedicated to 800G and 1.6T optical transceivers. https://t.co/yRbJMdeuxU

themes.topic: TSMC Q2 revenue reaches record high on AI chip demand
themes.summary: TSMC's Q2 revenue reached a record NT$1.27 trillion (US$40.07B), up 36% YoY and at the top end of guidance, driven by strong demand for AI chip production.

themes.topic: Pre-package screening becoming more valuable for advanced packaging
themes.summary: Rising economic value in advanced packages makes pre-package reliability screening, like wafer-level burn-in, increasingly valuable to improve effective advanced-packaging yield and reduce waste.

themes.topic: Wafer-level burn-in displacing package/system-level test spending
themes.summary: Wafer-level burn-in (WLBI) is gaining traction, with one customer eliminating system-level screening and another considering moving from package-level to wafer-level burn-in, potentially displacing traditional post-package test equipment.

themes.topic: Limited Nvidia H200 shipments to China/Hong Kong
themes.summary: Very few Nvidia H200 AI chips have shipped to China and Hong Kong despite license approvals, indicating a restart under new arrangements but with strict U.S. government review and compliance inspections.

themes.topic: Silicon Carbide (SiC) wafer prices rising due to AI/800V EV demand
themes.summary: Silicon Carbide (SiC) wafer prices are increasing due to rising demand for power semiconductors in AI data centers and 800V DC electric vehicles, leading to tight supplies and premium pricing for additional wafers.

mentions.text:
Prices of silicon carbide wafers (SiC) are warming up as demand picks up for power semiconductors used in AI data centers and 800V DC electric vehicles, media report, noting distributors say any client asking for additional SiC wafers must pay a premium due to tight supplies.

themes.topic: Samsung Electronics building new DRAM fab at Giheung
themes.summary: Samsung Electronics plans to build a new DRAM fab at its Giheung campus with a monthly capacity of 100,000 wafers, converting a former R&D site to meet surging AI-driven memory demand.
